en.extract:
The African shrike-flycatcher or red-eyed shrike-flycatcher (Megabyas flammulatus) is a species of bird in the family Vangidae. It is the only species in the monotypic genus Megabyas.


Distribution and habitat
It is widespread across the African tropical rainforest (including the Dahomey Gap).


References


External links
Image at ADW

The '''African shrike-flycatcher''' or '''red-eyed shrike-flycatcher''' ('''''Megabyas flammulatus''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Vangidae]].<ref>Louette, M. (2019). [https://www.hbw.com/species/african-shrike-flycatcher-megabyas-flammulatus "African Shrike-flycatcher (''Megabyas flammulatus'')"]. In: del Hoyo, J., Elliott, A., Sargatal, J., Christie, D. A. & de Juana, E. (eds.). ''Handbook of the Birds of the World Alive''. Lynx Edicions, Barcelona. Retrieved 25 November 2019.</ref> It is the only species in the [[monotypic]] genus '''''Megabyas'''''.

== Distribution and habitat ==
It is widespread across the [[Tropical rainforest|African tropical rainforest]] (including the [[Dahomey Gap]]).
